title: High bandwidth scalable wireless near-field interface
abstract: A transmitter or transceiver assembly includes at least one transmitter module. The transmitter module includes a matrix of transmitter integrated circuit die and a matrix of antennas, each antenna being coupled to a respective transmitter integrated circuit die. The matrix of antennas is configured to reduce interaction between signals transmitted by respective ones of the antennas.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed Is: 1. A communication assembly comprising: at least one module comprising: a printed circuit board; a group of integrated circuit die arranged on the printed circuit board, each die comprising a transmitter, a receiver or a transceiver; and a group of non-pianar antennas arranged on the printed circuit board, each antenna being coupled to a respective integrated circuit die; wherein the group of non-planar antennas is configured to reduce interaction between signals transmitted by respective ones of the antennas. 2. The communication assembly of claim 1, comprising a plurality of modules, module comprising: a group of integrated circuit die, each die comprising a transmitter, a receiver or a transceiver; and a group of antennas, each antenna being coupled to a respective integrated circuit die; wherein the group of antennas is configured to reduce interaction between signals transmitted by respective ones of the antennas. 3. The communication assembly of claim 1, wherein the group of antennas is configured to enhance communication between a transmitter and an intended target receiver. 4. The communication assembly of claim 3, wherein each of the integrated circuit die is arranged on a planar substrate. 5. The communication assembly of claim 1, wherein at least some of the integrated circuit die are stacked. 6. The communication assembly of claim 1, wherein at least some of the antennas are helical antennas. 7. The communication assembly of claim 1, wherein at least one the following: antennas or groups of antennas are at least partially physically isolated using isolation structures; antennas in closest proximity exhibit different polarization. the assembly is multi-channel with multiple frequency-separated channels, wherein antennas in proximity occupy different ones of the channels. 8. An antenna structure, comprising: a printed circuit board; and N helical millimeter wave antennas coupled to the printed circuit board 9. The antenna structure of claim 6, wherein N is 1 or more. 10. The antenna structure of claim 6, wherein N is 10 or more. 11. The antenna structure of claim 6, wherein N is 100 or more. 12. A wireless interface comprising: a first transmitter or transceiver matrix, comprising a matrix of first directional antennas and first isolation structures situated in proximity to the first directional antennas; and a second receiver or transceiver matrix, comprising a matrix of second directional antennas and isolation structures situated in proximity to the second directional antennas; wherein the first and second isolation structures are configured to be brought into contact proximity or contact with each other so as to align and couple respective ones of the first directional antennas with respective ones of the second directional antennas, and to isolate antennas that are aligned and coupled from other antennas. 13. A method of high-speed data transfer using a first matrix of transmitters or transceivers coupled to a first matrix of directional antennas, and a second matrix of receivers or transceivers coupled to a second matrix of directional antennas comprising: positioning the first matrix and second matrix of directional antennas to form pairs of antennas, antennas of a pair of antennas being positioned within a centimeter of each other and having respective axes of directionality that are substantially aligned; and employing one or more measures to at least partially isolate each pair of antennas from transmissions by other pairs of antennas. 14. A pick-and-place ready collection of surface-mount antennas 15. The collection of claim 14, wherein the antennas are helical antennas. 16. The collection of claim 14, wherein the antennas are configured to operate between 30 and 300 GHz. 17. The collection of claim 14, wherein the antennas are configured to operate within a range of 30 to 300 GHz. 18. A method of making an antenna matrix, dimensions of the antenna matrix being at least 2x2, comprising: robotically picking an antenna from a tape and reel of antennas; robotically placing the antenna in a surface-mount configuration on a printed circuit board; and repeating the picking and placing steps until the antenna matrix is complete. 19. A wireless communications device comprising: a printed circuit board; and 

 a matrix of antennas surface mounted on the printed circuit board, dimensions of the matrix being at least 2x2; wherein the antennas operate at a wavelength and are spaced apart by a distance in a range of 0 to 2 times the wavelength. 20. A wireless communications device, comprising: a printed circuit board; a matrix of wireless chips mounted on the PCB; and a matrix of antennas surface mounted on the printed circuit board, dimensions of the matrix being at least 2x2; wherein the antennas operate at a wavelength and are spaced apart by a distance in a range of 0 to 2 times the wavelength. 21. A wireless communications system comprising a first communication device and a second communication device, each of the first and second communication devices comprising a printed circuit board; a matrix of wireless chips mounted on the PCB; and a matrix of antennas surface mounted on the printed circuit board, dimensions of the matrix being at least 2x2; wherein the antennas operate at a wavelength and are spaced apart by a distance in a range of 0 to 2 times the wavelength. 22. The wireless communication system of claim 19, comprising a spacing and alignment mechanism for arranging the first and second communication devices in a desired arrangement for wireless communications. 23. The communication assembly of claim 1, wherein the group of antennas is situated on an opposite side of the printed circuit board than the group of non-planar antennas. 24. A communication assembly comprising: at least one module comprising: a group of integrated circuit die, each die comprising: a transmitter, a receiver or a transceiver; and at least one integrated millimeter-wave antenna. 25. The communication assembly of claim 4, wherein each of the integrated circuit die is arranged on a side of the planar substrate opposite a side on which the group of non-planar antennas is arranged on. 26. A surface mount millimeter wave helical antenna, comprising: a surface mount member; a conductive helical member held by the surface mount member and configured for millimeter wave transmission or reception; and a radome surrounding the helical member. 27. The antenna of claim 26, wherein the radome comprises a spacing feature for maintaining a separation distance between a wail of the radome and the helical member. 28. The antenna of claim 27, wherein the radome comprises a cylindrical body and a cap, wherein the separation feature is part of the cap. 29. The antenna of claim 28, wherein the separation feature is a taper extending away from a bottom side of the cap, configured to occupy a portion of a space inside the helical member. 30. The antenna of claim 26, wherein the radome is cylindrical.
